description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Flood and Learn doesn't work for random VNIs on 8325 and 8325P Switch series. Scenario: This issue can occur during a VSX upgrade with VXLAN replication mode set to underlay-multicast. Workaround: Reconfigure the impacted VNIs to restore back the flood and learn.

workaround: Reconfigure the impacted VNIs to restore back the flood and learn.

description: Symptom: Traffic loss occurs for IPv4 /32 or IPv6 /128 host-routes with matching directly connected hosts during certain network events. Scenario: This issue is most often seen when the switch reboots, but it may also occur when it initially starts up. Link-flaps could also trigger this issue. Workaround: Clear the ARP cache.
symptom: Traffic loss occurs for IPv4 /32 or IPv6 /128 host-routes with matching directly connected hosts during certain network events.

workaround: Clear the ARP cache.

description: (For 8325H Switch series only) Symptom: In a VLAN with a Virtual Active Gateway and DHCP relay, hosts will be unable to resolve a DHCP IP address received over a peer DHCP relay on an L2VNI. Scenario: A DHCP offer received over an L2VNI that is relayed by a VTEP peer will not be processed and gets dropped. Workaround: Configure a unique source IP (Loopback IP) for the DHCP relay agent that gets the DHCP offer on a L3VNI to the originating relay agent and disable DHCP relay for l2vpn-clients.
symptom: In a VLAN with a Virtual Active Gateway and DHCP relay, hosts will be unable to resolve a DHCP IP address received over a peer DHCP relay on an L2VNI.
scenario: A DHCP offer received over an L2VNI that is relayed by a VTEP peer will not be processed and gets dropped.
workaround: Configure a unique source IP (Loopback IP) for the DHCP relay agent that gets the DHCP offer on a L3VNI to the originating relay agent and disable DHCP relay for l2vpn-clients.

description: Symptom: On 8325P Switch series, PTP may not working after reboot of a PTP-unaware neighbor device on deployments with a large number of PTP clients (over 20 per port). Scenario: This issue will only be seen if the default PTP timers are modified and the device has a large number of PTP clients configured (over 20 per port). Workaround: For large scale PTP deployments it is recommended to keep the PTP timers set to their defaults.
symptom: On 8325P Switch series, PTP may not working after reboot of a PTP-unaware neighbor device on deployments with a large number of PTP clients (over 20 per port).

workaround: For large scale PTP deployments it is recommended to keep the PTP timers set to their defaults.
